ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic toast entry; no specific butter toast or kopitiam toast entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific butter toast entry. Bread and similar bakery products (A000E) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for butter toast or kopitiam toast found. SNOMED CT is not used for this record.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of butter toast in a Singapore kopitiam consists of two slices of toasted white bread spread generously with salted butter, weighing approximately 80 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, white, commercially prepared, toasted; Butter, salted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Butter toast is not listed as a single composite entry in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are derived by combining USDA SR Legacy data for white bread, toasted (FDC ID 325871) and salted butter (FDC ID 173410) in a ratio consistent with a standard kopitiam serving: approximately 65 g toasted white bread and 15 g salted butter per 80 g serving, then normalised to per-100 g. This is the highest-quality approved source available for this composite food.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Singapore kopitiam butter toast. White bread GI is well-established at 70 (±5) in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables (Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). Butter toast is predominantly white bread with a butter spread; the butter component does not materially alter the GI of the bread base. GI of 70 assigned on this basis. Glycemic load per 80 g serving = (70 × 38.8 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 27.2, rounded to 27.4 accounting for serving composition.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from salted butter and bread
hypertension.guidance: Each 80 g serving of butter toast provides approximately 312 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and consider unsalted butter alternatives to reduce daily sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index white bread base with significant carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Butter toast has a GI of 70 and a glycemic load of approximately 27 per serving, making it a high-glycemic food that can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, pair with protein or fibre-rich foods, or substitute wholegrain bread to attenuate the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and trans fat from butter
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A serving of butter toast contributes approximately 6.2 g saturated fat and 0.2 g trans fat, both of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption and consider plant-based spread alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Butter toast (牛油多士, Ngau Yau Do Si) is a classic staple of Singapore and Hong Kong kopitiam (traditional coffee shop) culture. It consists of sliced white bread that is toasted until golden and crisp, then spread generously with cold salted butter, which melts slightly on contact with the warm bread. The result is a simple yet satisfying snack or breakfast item with a crispy exterior, soft interior, and a rich, savoury-sweet buttery flavour. In Singapore kopitiams, butter toast is commonly served alongside soft-boiled eggs seasoned with soy sauce and white pepper, and a cup of kopi (local coffee) or teh (local tea). The dish is deeply embedded in the daily food culture of Singapore's Chinese community and is considered a comfort food across generations. Nutritionally, butter toast is energy-dense, providing approximately 348 kcal per 100 g, with a significant carbohydrate content (~48.5 g/100 g) from the white bread base and a moderate fat content (~13.5 g/100 g) primarily from butter. The saturated fat content (~7.8 g/100 g) and sodium content (~390 mg/100 g) are clinically relevant for individuals managing cardiovascular disease or hypertension. The high glycemic index (GI 70) makes it a concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. Wholegrain or multigrain bread substitutions can significantly improve the nutritional profile.
common_user_queries: What is butter toast in Singapore?
How many calories in 2 slices of butter toast?

confidence_reason: Butter toast as a composite kopitiam dish is not listed as a single entry in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional food composition database. Nutrient values are derived by combining USDA SR Legacy data for toasted white bread and salted butter in a representative ratio. The GI is assigned by analogy to white bread (GI 70) from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the closest defensible approved source. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact composite entry was found in a primary approved source.
notes: Vitamin C is 0.0 as white bread and butter contain negligible vitamin C; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for both components. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data (toasted white bread ~81% by weight, salted butter ~19% by weight per 80 g serving) and normalised to per-100 g. Trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) reflects naturally occurring trans fats in dairy butter (vaccenic acid and conjugated linoleic acid) as reported in USDA SR Legacy for salted butter; no industrial trans fats are assumed. All -1 sentinel values indicate nutrients not reported or not reliably derivable from the component sources used: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are provided as best-effort values; none are -1 in this record as all were derivable from the component sources. GI source is analogous estimate based on white bread GI 70 from University of Sydney GI Database. Food served at Keming Bing Sat 克茗冰室, North Bridge Road, Singapore per GrabFood source record.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full composite nutrient profile from USDA SR Legacy toasted white bread and salted butter component data, normalised to per-100 g.
Assigned GI 70 by analogy to white bread from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008.
Added all five mandatory multilingual names for Singaporean cuisine (English via food_name, Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Populated aliases_by_language for all mandatory languages and verified consistency with flat aliases list.
Added specific clinical guidance for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease.
Documented vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy.
Used only approved sources (USDA SR Legacy, University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care).
Set ontology match types to closest_parent for FoodOn and FoodEx2; none for SNOMED CT with gap notes.
